title: forsteronia gracilis
intro: Forsteronia gracilis, also known as the 'devil's breath', is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic effects. The health benefits of Forsteronia gracilis include pain relief, reduction of fever, and treatment of various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it can also be found in some commercial herbal supplements.
